name: CAP1188 Capacitive Touch Sensor
description: The `cap1188` sensor platform allows you to use your CAP1188 ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/datasheets/CAP1188.pdf), [Adafruit](https://learn.adafruit.com/adafruit-cap1188-breakout/overview)) Capacitive Touch Sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The I²C address of the sensor. Defaults to `0x29`.

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: Set the pin that is used to reset the CAP1188 board on boot.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Set the ID of this sensor.

config_entries.label: Allow Multiple Touches
config_entries.description: Whether to allow multitouch. Defaults to off.

config_entries.label: Touch Threshold
config_entries.description: The touch threshold for all channels. This defines the sensitivity for touch detection.
